pattern: Adjacent-swap with a shared popcount equivalence class lets each class sort internally; the array is sortable iff the maximum of each class stays below the minimum of every later class.
hints: Group consecutive elements that share the same number of set bits — elements within a group can be rearranged freely among themselves.
Within each group find the min and max; the array is invalid if any group's max exceeds the next group's min.
A single left-to-right pass over the groups is sufficient.

edge_inputs.description: two groups that can be sorted independently
impossible: boundary between groups in wrong order
all same popcount, any permutation allowed
two adjacent elements same popcount but need swap
elements with same popcount can cross group boundary visually but not functionally
descending order within one big group (sortable)

stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = min(size, 100)
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 255) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]

solutions.python:
class Solution:
    def canSortArray(self, nums: list) -> bool:
        n = len(nums)
        prev_max = 0
        i = 0
        while i < n:
            j = i
            pc = bin(nums[i]).count('1')
            while j < n and bin(nums[j]).count('1') == pc:
                j += 1
            seg_min = min(nums[i:j])
            seg_max = max(nums[i:j])
            if seg_min < prev_max:
                return False
            prev_max = seg_max
            i = j
        return True
